<pr_description>
[Feature]: Expose Generic Autofill Support via CDP (Autofill.trigger)
### Feature description

Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP) exposes the Autofill.trigger method, which allows triggering browser autofill programmatically using a single anchor field.

However, Puppeteer currently provides only limited support around autofill (mainly focused on credit card scenarios), and there is no clear high-level API that exposes the full capabilities of Autofill.trigger.

</pr_description>

     6	
     7	import type {Protocol} from 'devtools-protocol';

     9	import type {Frame} from '../api/Frame.js';
    10	import {getQueryHandlerAndSelector} from '../common/GetQueryHandler.js';
    11	import {LazyArg} from '../common/LazyArg.js';
    12	import type {
    13	  AwaitableIterable,
    14	  ElementFor,
    15	  EvaluateFuncWith,
    16	  HandleFor,
    17	  HandleOr,
    18	  NodeFor,
    19	} from '../common/types.js';
    20	import type {KeyInput} from '../common/USKeyboardLayout.js';
    21	import {isString, withSourcePuppeteerURLIfNone} from '../common/util.js';
    22	import {assert} from '../util/assert.js';
    23	import {AsyncIterableUtil} from '../util/AsyncIterableUtil.js';
    24	import {throwIfDisposed} from '../util/decorators.js';
    25	
    26	import {_isElementHandle} from './ElementHandleSymbol.js';
    27	import type {
    28	  KeyboardTypeOptions,
    29	  KeyPressOptions,
    30	  MouseClickOptions,
    31	  TouchHandle,
    32	} from './Input.js';
    33	import {JSHandle} from './JSHandle.js';

    36	import type {

    41	
    42	/**

   112	
   113	/**
   114	 * @public
   115	 */
   116	export interface ElementScreenshotOptions extends ScreenshotOptions {
   117	  /**
   118	   * @defaultValue `true`
   119	   */
   120	  scrollIntoView?: boolean;
   121	}
   122	
   123	/**
   124	 * A given method will have it's `this` replaced with an isolated version of
   125	 * `this` when decorated with this decorator.
   126	 *
   127	 * All changes of isolated `this` are reflected on the actual `this`.
   128	 *
   129	 * @internal
   130	 */
   131	export function bindIsolatedHandle<This extends ElementHandle<Node>>(
   132	  target: (this: This, ...args: any[]) => Promise<any>,
   133	  _: unknown,
   134	): typeof target {
   135	  return async function (...args) {
   136	    // If the handle is already isolated, then we don't need to adopt it
   137	    // again.
   138	    if (this.realm === this.frame.isolatedRealm()) {
   139	      return await target.call(this, ...args);
   140	    }
   141	    let adoptedThis: This;
   142	    if (this['isolatedHandle']) {
   143	      adoptedThis = this['isolatedHandle'];
   144	    } else {
   145	      this['isolatedHandle'] = adoptedThis = await this.frame
   146	        .isolatedRealm()
   147	        .adoptHandle(this);
   148	    }
   149	    const result = await target.call(adoptedThis, ...args);
   150	    // If the function returns `adoptedThis`, then we return `this`.
   151	    if (result === adoptedThis) {
   152	      return this;
   153	    }
   154	    // If the function returns a handle, transfer it into the current realm.
   155	    if (result instanceof JSHandle) {
   156	      return await this.realm.transferHandle(result);
   157	    }
   158	    // If the function returns an array of handlers, transfer them into the
   159	    // current realm.
   160	    if (Array.isArray(result)) {
   161	      await Promise.all(
   162	        result.map(async (item, index, result) => {
   163	          if (item instanceof JSHandle) {
   164	            result[index] = await this.realm.transferHandle(item);
   165	          }
   166	        }),
   167	      );
   168	    }
   169	    if (result instanceof Map) {
   170	      await Promise.all(
   171	        [...result.entries()].map(async ([key, value]) => {
   172	          if (value instanceof JSHandle) {
   173	            result.set(key, await this.realm.transferHandle(value));
   174	          }
   175	        }),
   176	      );
   177	    }
   178	    return result;
   179	  };
   180	}
   181	
   182	/**
   183	 * ElementHandle represents an in-page DOM element.
   184	 *
   185	 * @remarks
   186	 * ElementHandles can be created with the {@link Page.$} method.
   187	 *
   188	 * ```ts
   189	 * import puppeteer from 'puppeteer';
   190	 *
   191	 * const browser = await puppeteer.launch();
   192	 * const page = await browser.newPage();
   193	 * await page.goto('https://example.com');
   194	 * const hrefElement = await page.$('a');
   195	 * await hrefElement.click();
   196	 * // ...
   197	 * ```
   198	 *
   199	 * ElementHandle prevents the DOM element from being garbage-collected unless the
   200	 * handle is {@link JSHandle.dispose | disposed}. ElementHandles are auto-disposed
   201	 * when their associated frame is navigated away or the parent
   202	 * context gets destroyed.
   203	 *
   204	 * ElementHandle instances can be used as arguments in {@link Page.$eval} and
   205	 * {@link Page.evaluate} methods.
   206	 *
   207	 * If you're using TypeScript, ElementHandle takes a generic argument that
   208	 * denotes the type of element the handle is holding within. For example, if you
   209	 * have a handle to a `<select>` element, you can type it as
   210	 * `ElementHandle<HTMLSelectElement>` and you get some nicer type checks.
   211	 *
   212	 * @public
   213	 */
   214	export abstract class ElementHandle<
   215	  ElementType extends Node = Element,
   216	> extends JSHandle<ElementType> {
   217	  /**
   218	   * @internal
   219	   */
   220	  declare [_isElementHandle]: boolean;
   221	
   222	  /**
   223	   * @internal
   224	   * Cached isolatedHandle to prevent
   225	   * trying to adopt it multiple times
   226	   */
   227	  isolatedHandle?: typeof this;
   228	
   229	  /**
   230	   * @internal
   231	   */
   232	  protected readonly handle;
   233	
   234	  /**
   235	   * @internal
   236	   */
   237	  constructor(handle: JSHandle<ElementType>) {
   238	    super();
   239	    this.handle = handle;
   240	    this[_isElementHandle] = true;
   241	  }
   242	
   243	  /**
   244	   * @internal
   245	   */
   246	  override get id(): string | undefined {
   247	    return this.handle.id;
   248	  }
   249	
   250	  /**
   251	   * @internal
   252	   */
   253	  override get disposed(): boolean {
   254	    return this.handle.disposed;
   255	  }
   256	
   257	  /**
   258	   * @internal
   259	   */
   260	  @throwIfDisposed()
   261	  @bindIsolatedHandle
   262	  override async getProperty<K extends keyof ElementType>(
   263	    propertyName: HandleOr<K>,
   264	  ): Promise<HandleFor<ElementType[K]>> {
   265	    return await this.handle.getProperty(propertyName);
   266	  }
   267	
   268	  /**
   269	   * @internal
   270	   */
   271	  @throwIfDisposed()
   272	  @bindIsolatedHandle
   273	  override async getProperties(): Promise<Map<string, JSHandle>> {
   274	    return await this.handle.getProperties();
   275	  }
   276	
   277	  /**
   278	   * @internal
   279	   */
   280	  override async evaluate<
   281	    Params extends unknown[],
   282	    Func extends EvaluateFuncWith<ElementType, Params> = EvaluateFuncWith<
   283	      ElementType,
   284	      Params
   285	    >,
   286	  >(
   287	    pageFunction: Func | string,
   288	    ...args: Params
   289	  ): Promise<Awaited<ReturnType<Func>>> {
   290	    pageFunction = withSourcePuppeteerURLIfNone(
   291	      this.evaluate.name,
   292	      pageFunction,
   293	    );
   294	    return await this.handle.evaluate(pageFunction, ...args);
   295	  }
   296	
   297	  /**
   298	   * @internal
   299	   */
   300	  override async evaluateHandle<
   301	    Params extends unknown[],
   302	    Func extends EvaluateFuncWith<ElementType, Params> = EvaluateFuncWith<
   303	      ElementType,
   304	      Params
   305	    >,
   306	  >(
   307	    pageFunction: Func | string,
   308	    ...args: Params
   309	  ): Promise<HandleFor<Awaited<ReturnType<Func>>>> {
   310	    pageFunction = withSourcePuppeteerURLIfNone(
   311	      this.evaluateHandle.name,
   312	      pageFunction,
   313	    );
   314	    return await this.handle.evaluateHandle(pageFunction, ...args);
   315	  }
   316	
   317	  /**
   318	   * @internal
   319	   */
   320	  @throwIfDisposed()
   321	  @bindIsolatedHandle
   322	  override async jsonValue(): Promise<ElementType> {
   323	    return await this.handle.jsonValue();
   324	  }
   325	
   326	  /**
   327	   * @internal
   328	   */
   329	  override toString(): string {
   330	    return this.handle.toString();
   331	  }
   332	
   333	  /**
   334	   * @internal
   335	   */
   336	  override remoteObject(): Protocol.Runtime.RemoteObject {
   337	    return this.handle.remoteObject();
   338	  }
   339	
   340	  /**
   341	   * @internal
   342	   */
   343	  override async dispose(): Promise<void> {
   344	    await Promise.all([this.handle.dispose(), this.isolatedHandle?.dispose()]);
   345	  }
   346	
   347	  /**
   348	   * @internal
   349	   */
   350	  override asElement(): ElementHandle<ElementType> {
   351	    return this;
   352	  }
   353	
   354	  /**
   355	   * Frame corresponding to the current handle.
   356	   */
   357	  abstract get frame(): Frame;
   358	
   359	  /**
   360	   * Queries the current element for an element matching the given selector.
   361	   *
   362	   * @param selector -
   363	   * {@link https://pptr.dev/guides/page-interactions#selectors | selector}
   364	   * to query the page for.
   365	   * {@link https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/CSS_Selectors | CSS selectors}
   366	   * can be passed as-is and a
   367	   * {@link https://pptr.dev/guides/page-interactions#non-css-selectors | Puppeteer-specific selector syntax}
   368	   * allows querying by
   369	   * {@link https://pptr.dev/guides/page-interactions#text-selectors--p-text | text},
   370	   * {@link https://pptr.dev/guides/page-interactions#aria-selectors--p-aria | a11y role and name},
   371	   * and
   372	   * {@link https://pptr.dev/guides/page-interactions#xpath-selectors--p-xpath | xpath}
   373	   * and
   374	   * {@link https://pptr.dev/guides/page-interactions#querying-elements-in-shadow-dom | combining these queries across shadow roots}.
   375	   * Alternatively, you can specify the selector type using a
   376	   * {@link https://pptr.dev/guides/page-interactions#prefixed-selector-syntax | prefix}.
   377	   * @returns A {@link ElementHandle | element handle} to the first element
   378	   * matching the given selector. Otherwise, `null`.
   379	   */
   380	  @throwIfDisposed()
   381	  @bindIsolatedHandle
   382	  async $<Selector extends string>(
   383	    selector: Selector,
   384	  ): Promise<ElementHandle<NodeFor<Selector>> | null> {
   385	    const {updatedSelector, QueryHandler} =
   386	      getQueryHandlerAndSelector(selector);
   387	    return (await QueryHandler.queryOne(
   388	      this,
   389	      updatedSelector,
   390	    )) as ElementHandle<NodeFor<Selector>> | null;
   391	  }
   392	
   393	  /**
   394	   * Queries the current element for all elements matching the given selector.
   395	   *
   396	   * @param selector -
   397	   * {@link https://pptr.dev/guides/page-interactions#selectors | selector}
   398	   * to query the page for.
   399	   * {@link https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/CSS_Selectors | CSS selectors}
   400	   * can be passed as-is and a
   401	   * {@link https://pptr.dev/guides/page-interactions#non-css-selectors | Puppeteer-specific selector syntax}
   402	   * allows querying by
   403	   * {@link https://pptr.dev/guides/page-interactions#text-selectors--p-text | text},
   404	   * {@link https://pptr.dev/guides/page-interactions#aria-selectors--p-aria | a11y role and name},
   405	   * and
   406	   * {@link https://pptr.dev/guides/page-interactions#xpath-selectors--p-xpath | xpath}
   407	   * and
   408	   * {@link https://pptr.dev/guides/page-interactions#querying-elements-in-shadow-dom | combining these queries across shadow roots}.
   409	   * Alternatively, you can specify the selector type using a
   410	   * {@link https://pptr.dev/guides/page-interactions#prefixed-selector-syntax | prefix}.
   411	   * @returns An array of {@link ElementHandle | element handles} that point to
   412	   * elements matching the given selector.
   413	   */
   414	  @throwIfDisposed()
   415	  async $$<Selector extends string>(
   416	    selector: Selector,
   417	    options?: QueryOptions,
   418	  ): Promise<Array<ElementHandle<NodeFor<Selector>>>> {
   419	    if (options?.isolate === false) {
   420	      return await this.#$$impl(selector);
   421	    }
   422	    return await this.#$$(selector);
   423	  }
   424	
   425	  /**
   426	   * Isolates {@link ElementHandle.$$} if needed.
   427	   *
   428	   * @internal
   429	   */
   430	  @bindIsolatedHandle
   431	  async #$$<Selector extends string>(
   432	    selector: Selector,
   433	  ): Promise<Array<ElementHandle<NodeFor<Selector>>>> {
   434	    return await this.#$$impl(selector);
   435	  }
   436	
   437	  /**
   438	   * Implementation for {@link ElementHandle.$$}.
   439	   *
   440	   * @internal
   441	   */
   442	  async #$$impl<Selector extends string>(
   443	    selector: Selector,
   444	  ): Promise<Array<ElementHandle<NodeFor<Selector>>>> {
   445	    const {updatedSelector, QueryHandler} =
   446	      getQueryHandlerAndSelector(selector);
   447	    return await (AsyncIterableUtil.collect(
   448	      QueryHandler.queryAll(this, updatedSelector),
   449	    ) as Promise<Array<ElementHandle<NodeFor<Selector>>>>);
   450	  }
   451	
   452	  /**
   453	   * Runs the given function on the first element matching the given selector in
   454	   * the current element.
   455	   *
   456	   * If the given function returns a promise, then this method will wait till
   457	   * the promise resolves.
   458	   *
   459	   * @example
   460	   *
   461	   * ```ts
   462	   * const tweetHandle = await page.$('.tweet');
   463	   * expect(await tweetHandle.$eval('.like', node => node.innerText)).toBe(
   464	   *   '100',
   465	   * );
   466	   * expect(await tweetHandle.$eval('.retweets', node => node.innerText)).toBe(
   467	   *   '10',
   468	   * );
   469	   * ```
   470	   *
   471	   * @param selector -
   472	   * {@link https://pptr.dev/guides/page-interactions#selectors | selector}
   473	   * to query the page for.
   474	   * {@link https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/CSS_Selectors | CSS selectors}
   475	   * can be passed as-is and a
   476	   * {@link https://pptr.dev/guides/page-interactions#non-css-selectors | Puppeteer-specific selector syntax}
   477	   * allows querying by
   478	   * {@link https://pptr.dev/guides/page-interactions#text-selectors--p-text | text},
   479	   * {@link https://pptr.dev/guides/page-interactions#aria-selectors--p-aria | a11y role and name},
   480	   * and
   481	   * {@link https://pptr.dev/guides/page-interactions#xpath-selectors--p-xpath | xpath}
   482	   * and
   483	   * {@link https://pptr.dev/guides/page-interactions#querying-elements-in-shadow-dom | combining these queries across shadow roots}.
   484	   * Alternatively, you can specify the selector type using a
   485	   * {@link https://pptr.dev/guides/page-interactions#prefixed-selector-syntax | prefix}.
   486	   * @param pageFunction - The function to be evaluated in this element's page's
   487	   * context. The first element matching the selector will be passed in as the
   488	   * first argument.
   489	   * @param args - Additional arguments to pass to `pageFunction`.
   490	   * @returns A promise to the result of the function.
   491	   */
   492	  async $eval<
   493	    Selector extends string,
   494	    Params extends unknown[],
   495	    Func extends EvaluateFuncWith<NodeFor<Selector>, Params> = EvaluateFuncWith<
   496	      NodeFor<Selector>,
   497	      Params
   498	    >,
   499	  >(
   500	    selector: Selector,
   501	    pageFunction: Func | string,
   502	    ...args: Params
   503	  ): Promise<Awaited<ReturnType<Func>>> {
   504	    pageFunction = withSourcePuppeteerURLIfNone(this.$eval.name, pageFunction);
   505	    using elementHandle = await this.$(selector);
   506	    if (!elementHandle) {
   507	      throw new Error(
   508	        `Error: failed to find element matching selector "${selector}"`,
   509	      );
   510	    }
   511	    return await elementHandle.evaluate(pageFunction, ...args);
   512	  }
   513	
   514	  /**
   515	   * Runs the given function on an array of elements matching the given selector

   520	   *
   521	   * @example
   522	   * HTML:
   523	   *
   524	   * ```html
   525	   * <div class="feed">
   526	   *   <div class="tweet">Hello!</div>

  1597	  /**
  1598	   * If the element is a form input, you can use {@link ElementHandle.autofill}
  1599	   * to test if the form is compatible with the browser's autofill
  1600	   * implementation. Throws an error if the form cannot be autofilled.
  1601	   *
  1602	   * @remarks
  1603	   *
  1604	   * Currently, Puppeteer supports auto-filling credit card information only and
  1605	   * in Chrome in the new headless and headful modes only.
  1606	   *
  1607	   * ```ts
  1608	   * // Select an input on the credit card form.
  1609	   * const name = await page.waitForSelector('form #name');
  1610	   * // Trigger autofill with the desired data.
  1611	   * await name.autofill({
  1612	   *   creditCard: {
  1613	   *     number: '4444444444444444',
  1614	   *     name: 'John Smith',
  1615	   *     expiryMonth: '01',
  1616	   *     expiryYear: '2030',
  1617	   *     cvc: '123',
  1618	   *   },
  1619	   * });
  1620	   * ```
  1621	   */
  1622	  abstract autofill(data: AutofillData): Promise<void>;
  1623

  1625	   * When connected using Chrome DevTools Protocol, it returns a
  1626	   * DOM.BackendNodeId for the element.
  1627	   */

     1	/**
     2	 * @license
     3	 * Copyright 2019 Google Inc.
     4	 * SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
     5	 */
     6	
     7	import type {Protocol} from 'devtools-protocol';
     8	
     9	import type {CDPSession} from '../api/CDPSession.js';
    10	import {
    11	  bindIsolatedHandle,
    12	  ElementHandle,
    13	  type AutofillData,
    14	} from '../api/ElementHandle.js';
    15	import type {AwaitableIterable} from '../common/types.js';
    16	import {debugError} from '../common/util.js';
    17	import {environment} from '../environment.js';
    18	import {assert} from '../util/assert.js';
    19	import {AsyncIterableUtil} from '../util/AsyncIterableUtil.js';
    20	import {throwIfDisposed} from '../util/decorators.js';
    21	
    22	import type {CdpFrame} from './Frame.js';
    23	import type {FrameManager} from './FrameManager.js';
    24	import type {IsolatedWorld} from './IsolatedWorld.js';
    25	import {CdpJSHandle} from './JSHandle.js';
    26	
    27	const NON_ELEMENT_NODE_ROLES = new Set(['StaticText', 'InlineTextBox']);
    28	
    29	/**
    30	 * The CdpElementHandle extends ElementHandle now to keep compatibility
    31	 * with `instanceof` because of that we need to have methods for
    32	 * CdpJSHandle to in this implementation as well.
    33	 *
    34	 * @internal
    35	 */
    36	export class CdpElementHandle<
    37	  ElementType extends Node = Element,
    38	> extends ElementHandle<ElementType> {
    39	  declare protected readonly handle: CdpJSHandle<ElementType>;
    40	  #backendNodeId?: number;
    41	
    42	  constructor(
    43	    world: IsolatedWorld,
    44	    remoteObject: Protocol.Runtime.RemoteObject,
    45	  ) {
    46	    super(new CdpJSHandle(world, remoteObject));
    47	  }
    48	
    49	  override get realm(): IsolatedWorld {
    50	    return this.handle.realm;
    51	  }
    52	
    53	  get client(): CDPSession {
    54	    return this.handle.client;
    55	  }
    56	
    57	  override remoteObject(): Protocol.Runtime.RemoteObject {
    58	    return this.handle.remoteObject();
    59	  }
    60	
    61	  get #frameManager(): FrameManager {
    62	    return this.frame._frameManager;
    63	  }
    64	
    65	  override get frame(): CdpFrame {
    66	    return this.realm.environment as CdpFrame;
    67	  }
    68	
    69	  override async contentFrame(
    70	    this: ElementHandle<HTMLIFrameElement>,
    71	  ): Promise<CdpFrame>;
    72	
    73	  @throwIfDisposed()
    74	  override async contentFrame(): Promise<CdpFrame | null> {
    75	    const nodeInfo = await this.client.send('DOM.describeNode', {
    76	      objectId: this.id,
    77	    });
    78	    if (typeof nodeInfo.node.frameId !== 'string') {
    79	      return null;
    80	    }
    81	    return this.#frameManager.frame(nodeInfo.node.frameId);
    82	  }
    83	
    84	  @throwIfDisposed()
    85	  @bindIsolatedHandle
    86	  override async scrollIntoView(
    87	    this: CdpElementHandle<Element>,
    88	  ): Promise<void> {
    89	    await this.assertConnectedElement();
    90	    try {
    91	      await this.client.send('DOM.scrollIntoViewIfNeeded', {
    92	        objectId: this.id,
    93	      });
    94	    } catch (error) {
    95	      debugError(error);
    96	      // Fallback to Element.scrollIntoView if DOM.scrollIntoViewIfNeeded is not supported
    97	      await super.scrollIntoView();
    98	    }
    99	  }
   100	
   101	  @throwIfDisposed()
   102	  @bindIsolatedHandle
   103	  override async uploadFile(
   104	    this: CdpElementHandle<HTMLInputElement>,
   105	    ...files: string[]
   106	  ): Promise<void> {
   107	    const isMultiple = await this.evaluate(element => {
   108	      return element.multiple;
   109	    });
   110	    assert(
   111	      files.length <= 1 || isMultiple,
   112	      'Multiple file uploads only work with <input type=file multiple>',
   113	    );
   114	
   115	    // Locate all files and confirm that they exist.
   116	    const path = environment.value.path;
   117	    if (path) {
   118	      files = files.map(filePath => {
   119	        if (
   120	          path.win32.isAbsolute(filePath) ||
   121	          path.posix.isAbsolute(filePath)
   122	        ) {
   123	          return filePath;
   124	        } else {
   125	          return path.resolve(filePath);
   126	        }
   127	      });
   128	    }
   129	
   130	    /**
   131	     * The zero-length array is a special case, it seems that
   132	     * DOM.setFileInputFiles does not actually update the files in that case, so
   133	     * the solution is to eval the element value to a new FileList directly.
   134	     */
   135	    if (files.length === 0) {
   136	      // XXX: These events should converted to trusted events. Perhaps do this
   137	      // in `DOM.setFileInputFiles`?
   138	      await this.evaluate(element => {
   139	        element.files = new DataTransfer().files;
   140	
   141	        // Dispatch events for this case because it should behave akin to a user action.
   142	        element.dispatchEvent(
   143	          new Event('input', {bubbles: true, composed: true}),
   144	        );
   145	        element.dispatchEvent(new Event('change', {bubbles: true}));
   146	      });
   147	      return;
   148	    }
   149	
   150	    const {
   151	      node: {backendNodeId},
   152	    } = await this.client.send('DOM.describeNode', {
   153	      objectId: this.id,
   154	    });
   155	    await this.client.send('DOM.setFileInputFiles', {
   156	      objectId: this.id,
   157	      files,
   158	      backendNodeId,
   159	    });
   160	  }
   161	
   162	  @throwIfDisposed()
   163	  override async autofill(data: AutofillData): Promise<void> {
   164	    const nodeInfo = await this.client.send('DOM.describeNode', {
   165	      objectId: this.handle.id,
   166	    });
   167	    const fieldId = nodeInfo.node.backendNodeId;
   168	    const frameId = this.frame._id;
   169	    await this.client.send('Autofill.trigger', {
   170	      fieldId,
   171	      frameId,
   172	      card: data.creditCard,
   173	    });
   174	  }
   175	
   176	  override async *queryAXTree(
   177	    name?: string | undefined,
   178	    role?: string | undefined,
   179	  ): AwaitableIterable<ElementHandle<Node>> {
   180	    const {nodes} = await this.client.send('Accessibility.queryAXTree', {
   181	      objectId: this.id,
   182	      accessibleName: name,
   183	      role,
   184	    });
   185	
   186	    const results = nodes.filter(node => {
   187	      if (node.ignored) {
   188	        return false;
   189	      }
   190	      if (!node.role) {
   191	        return false;
   192	      }
   193	      if (NON_ELEMENT_NODE_ROLES.has(node.role.value)) {
   194	        return false;
   195	      }
   196	      return true;
   197	    });
   198	
   199	    return yield* AsyncIterableUtil.map(results, node => {
   200	      return this.realm.adoptBackendNode(node.backendDOMNodeId) as Promise<
   201	        ElementHandle<Node>
   202	      >;
   203	    });
   204	  }
   205	
   206	  override async backendNodeId(): Promise<number> {
   207	    if (this.#backendNodeId) {
   208	      return this.#backendNodeId;
   209	    }
   210	    const {node} = await this.client.send('DOM.describeNode', {
   211	      objectId: this.handle.id,
   212	    });
   213	    this.#backendNodeId = node.backendNodeId;
   214	    return this.#backendNodeId;
   215	  }
   216	}
   217

     1	/**
     2	 * @license
     3	 * Copyright 2023 Google Inc.
     4	 * SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
     5	 */
     6	
     7	import type * as Bidi from 'webdriver-bidi-protocol';
     8	
     9	import {
    10	  bindIsolatedHandle,
    11	  ElementHandle,
    12	  type AutofillData,
    13	} from '../api/ElementHandle.js';
    14	import {UnsupportedOperation} from '../common/Errors.js';
    15	import type {AwaitableIterable} from '../common/types.js';
    16	import {environment} from '../environment.js';
    17	import {AsyncIterableUtil} from '../util/AsyncIterableUtil.js';
    18	import {throwIfDisposed} from '../util/decorators.js';
    19	
    20	import type {BidiFrame} from './Frame.js';
    21	import {BidiJSHandle} from './JSHandle.js';
    22	import type {BidiFrameRealm} from './Realm.js';
    23	
    24	/**
    25	 * @internal
    26	 */
    27	export class BidiElementHandle<
    28	  ElementType extends Node = Element,
    29	> extends ElementHandle<ElementType> {
    30	  #backendNodeId?: number;
    31	
    32	  static from<ElementType extends Node = Element>(
    33	    value: Bidi.Script.RemoteValue,
    34	    realm: BidiFrameRealm,
    35	  ): BidiElementHandle<ElementType> {
    36	    return new BidiElementHandle(value, realm);
    37	  }
    38	
    39	  declare handle: BidiJSHandle<ElementType>;
    40	
    41	  constructor(value: Bidi.Script.RemoteValue, realm: BidiFrameRealm) {
    42	    super(BidiJSHandle.from(value, realm));
    43	  }
    44	
    45	  override get realm(): BidiFrameRealm {
    46	    // SAFETY: See the super call in the constructor.
    47	    return this.handle.realm as BidiFrameRealm;
    48	  }
    49	
    50	  override get frame(): BidiFrame {
    51	    return this.realm.environment;
    52	  }
    53	
    54	  remoteValue(): Bidi.Script.RemoteValue {
    55	    return this.handle.remoteValue();
    56	  }
    57	
    58	  @throwIfDisposed()
    59	  override async autofill(data: AutofillData): Promise<void> {
    60	    const client = this.frame.client;
    61	    const nodeInfo = await client.send('DOM.describeNode', {
    62	      objectId: this.handle.id,
    63	    });
    64	    const fieldId = nodeInfo.node.backendNodeId;
    65	    const frameId = this.frame._id;
    66	    await client.send('Autofill.trigger', {
    67	      fieldId,
    68	      frameId,
    69	      card: data.creditCard,
    70	    });
    71	  }
    72	
    73	  override async contentFrame(
    74	    this: BidiElementHandle<HTMLIFrameElement>,
    75	  ): Promise<BidiFrame>;
    76	  @throwIfDisposed()
    77	  @bindIsolatedHandle
    78	  override async contentFrame(): Promise<BidiFrame | null> {
    79	    using handle = (await this.evaluateHandle(element => {
    80	      if (
    81	        element instanceof HTMLIFrameElement ||
    82	        element instanceof HTMLFrameElement
    83	      ) {
    84	        return element.contentWindow;
    85	      }
    86	      return;
    87	    })) as BidiJSHandle;
    88	    const value = handle.remoteValue();
    89	    if (value.type === 'window') {
    90	      return (
    91	        this.frame
    92	          .page()
    93	          .frames()
    94	          .find(frame => {
    95	            return frame._id === value.value.context;
    96	          }) ?? null
    97	      );
    98	    }
    99	    return null;
   100	  }
   101	
   102	  override async uploadFile(
   103	    this: BidiElementHandle<HTMLInputElement>,
   104	    ...files: string[]
   105	  ): Promise<void> {
   106	    // Locate all files and confirm that they exist.
   107	    const path = environment.value.path;
   108	    if (path) {
   109	      files = files.map(file => {
   110	        if (path.win32.isAbsolute(file) || path.posix.isAbsolute(file)) {
   111	          return file;
   112	        } else {
   113	          return path.resolve(file);
   114	        }
   115	      });
   116	    }
   117	    await this.frame.setFiles(this, files);
   118	  }
   119	
   120	  override async *queryAXTree(
   121	    this: BidiElementHandle<HTMLElement>,
   122	    name?: string | undefined,
   123	    role?: string | undefined,
   124	  ): AwaitableIterable<ElementHandle<Node>> {
   125	    const results = await this.frame.locateNodes(this, {
   126	      type: 'accessibility',
   127	      value: {
   128	        role,
   129	        name,
   130	      },
   131	    });
   132	
   133	    return yield* AsyncIterableUtil.map(results, node => {
   134	      // TODO: maybe change ownership since the default ownership is probably none.
   135	      return Promise.resolve(BidiElementHandle.from(node, this.realm));
   136	    });
   137	  }
   138	
   139	  override async backendNodeId(): Promise<number> {
   140	    if (!this.frame.page().browser().cdpSupported) {
   141	      throw new UnsupportedOperation();
   142	    }
   143	    if (this.#backendNodeId) {
   144	      return this.#backendNodeId;
   145	    }
   146	    const {node} = await this.frame.client.send('DOM.describeNode', {
   147	      objectId: this.handle.id,
   148	    });
   149	    this.#backendNodeId = node.backendNodeId;
   150	    return this.#backendNodeId;
   151	  }
   152	}
   153

    44	/**
    45	 * @public
    46	 */
    47	export interface WaitForOptions {
    48	  /**
    49	   * Maximum wait time in milliseconds. Pass 0 to disable the timeout.
    50	   *
    51	   * The default value can be changed by using the
    52	   * {@link Page.setDefaultTimeout} or {@link Page.setDefaultNavigationTimeout}
    53	   * methods.
    54	   *
    55	   * @defaultValue `30000`
    56	   */
    57	  timeout?: number;
    58	  /**
    59	   * When to consider waiting succeeds. Given an array of event strings, waiting
    60	   * is considered to be successful after all events have been fired.
    61	   *
    62	   * @defaultValue `'load'`
    63	   */
    64	  waitUntil?: PuppeteerLifeCycleEvent | PuppeteerLifeCycleEvent[];
    65	  /**
    66	   * @internal
    67	   */
    68	  ignoreSameDocumentNavigation?: boolean;
    69	  /**
    70	   * A signal object that allows you to cancel the call.
    71	   */
    72	  signal?: AbortSignal;
    73	}
    74	
    75	/**
    76	 * @public
    77	 */
    78	export interface GoToOptions extends WaitForOptions {
    79	  /**
    80	   * If provided, it will take preference over the referer header value set by
    81	   * {@link Page.setExtraHTTPHeaders | page.setExtraHTTPHeaders()}.
    82	   */
    83	  referer?: string;
    84	  /**
    85	   * If provided, it will take preference over the referer-policy header value
    86	   * set by {@link Page.setExtraHTTPHeaders | page.setExtraHTTPHeaders()}.
    87	   */
    88	  referrerPolicy?: string;
    89	}
    90	
    91	/**
    92	 * @public
    93	 */
    94	export interface FrameWaitForFunctionOptions {
    95	  /**
    96	   * An interval at which the `pageFunction` is executed, defaults to `raf`. If
    97	   * `polling` is a number, then it is treated as an interval in milliseconds at
    98	   * which the function would be executed. If `polling` is a string, then it can
    99	   * be one of the following values:
   100	   *
   101	   * - `raf` - to constantly execute `pageFunction` in `requestAnimationFrame`
   102	   *   callback. This is the tightest polling mode which is suitable to observe
   103	   *   styling changes.
   104	   *
   105	   * - `mutation` - to execute `pageFunction` on every DOM mutation.
   106	   */
   107	  polling?: 'raf' | 'mutation' | number;
   108	  /**
   109	   * Maximum time to wait in milliseconds. Defaults to `30000` (30 seconds).
   110	   * Pass `0` to disable the timeout. Puppeteer's default timeout can be changed
   111	   * using {@link Page.setDefaultTimeout}.
   112	   */
   113	  timeout?: number;
   114	  /**
   115	   * A signal object that allows you to cancel a waitForFunction call.
   116	   */
   117	  signal?: AbortSignal;
   118	}
   119

   124	  /**

   126	   */

   128	  /**
   129	   * Path to a JavaScript file to be injected into the frame.
   130	   *
   131	   * @remarks
   132	   * If `path` is a relative path, it is resolved relative to the current
   133	   * working directory (`process.cwd()` in Node.js).
   134	   */
   135	  path?: string;
   136	  /**
   137	   * JavaScript to be injected into the frame.
   138	   */
   139	  content?: string;
   140	  /**
   141	   * Sets the `type` of the script. Use `module` in order to load an ES2015 module.
   142	   */
   143	  type?: string;
   144	  /**
   145	   * Sets the `id` of the script.
   146	   */
   147	  id?: string;
   148	}

   157	  url?: string;
   158	  /**

   163	   */
   164	  path?: string;

   168	  content?: string;

   184	  [FrameEvent.FrameDetached]: Frame;
   185	  /** @internal */
   186	  [FrameEvent.FrameSwappedByActivation]: undefined;
   187	}
   188	
   189	/**
   190	 * We use symbols to prevent external parties listening to these events.
   191	 * They are internal to Puppeteer.
   192	 *
   193	 * @internal
   194	 */
   195	// eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-namespace
   196	export namespace FrameEvent {
   197	  export const FrameNavigated = Symbol('Frame.FrameNavigated');
   198	  export const FrameSwapped = Symbol('Frame.FrameSwapped');
   199	  export const LifecycleEvent = Symbol('Frame.LifecycleEvent');
   200	  export const FrameNavigatedWithinDocument = Symbol(
   201	    'Frame.FrameNavigatedWithinDocument',
   202	  );
   203	  export const FrameDetached = Symbol('Frame.FrameDetached');
   204	  export const FrameSwappedByActivation = Symbol(
   205	    'Frame.FrameSwappedByActivation',
   206	  );
   207	}
   208	
   209	/**
   210	 * @internal
   211	 */
   212	export const throwIfDetached = throwIfDisposed<Frame>(frame => {
   213	  return `Attempted to use detached Frame '${frame._id}'.`;
   214	});
   215	
   216	/**
   217	 * Represents a DOM frame.
   218	 *
   219	 * To understand frames, you can think of frames as `<iframe>` elements. Just
   220	 * like iframes, frames can be nested, and when JavaScript is executed in a
   221	 * frame, the JavaScript does not affect frames inside the ambient frame the
   222	 * JavaScript executes in.
   223	 *
   224	 * @example
   225	 * At any point in time, {@link Page | pages} expose their current frame
   226	 * tree via the {@link Page.mainFrame} and {@link Frame.childFrames} methods.
   227	 *
   228	 * @example
   229	 * An example of dumping frame tree:
   230	 *
   231	 * ```ts
   232	 * import puppeteer from 'puppeteer';
   233	 *
   234	 * const browser = await puppeteer.launch();
   235	 * const page = await browser.newPage();
   236	 * await page.goto('https://www.google.com/chrome/browser/canary.html');
   237	 * dumpFrameTree(page.mainFrame(), '');
   238	 * await browser.close();
   239	 *
   240	 * function dumpFrameTree(frame, indent) {
   241	 *   console.log(indent + frame.url());
   242	 *   for (const child of frame.childFrames()) {
   243	 *     dumpFrameTree(child, indent + '  ');
   244	 *   }
   245	 * }
   246	 * ```
   247	 *
   248	 * @example
   249	 * An example of getting text from an iframe element:
   250	 *
   251	 * ```ts
   252	 * const frames = page.frames();
   253	 * let frame = null;
   254	 * for (const currentFrame of frames) {
   255	 *   const frameElement = await currentFrame.frameElement();
   256	 *   const name = await frameElement.evaluate(el => el.getAttribute('name'));
   257	 *   if (name === 'myframe') {
   258	 *     frame = currentFrame;
   259	 *     break;
   260	 *   }
   261	 * }
   262	 * if (frame) {
   263	 *   const text = await frame.$eval(
   264	 *     '.selector',
   265	 *     element => element.textContent,
   266	 *   );
   267	 *   console.log(text);
   268	 * } else {
   269	 *   console.error('Frame with name "myframe" not found.');
   270	 * }
   271	 * ```
   272	 *
   273	 * @remarks
   274	 * Frame lifecycles are controlled by three events that are all dispatched on
   275	 * the parent {@link Frame.page | page}:
   276	 *
   277	 * - {@link PageEvent.FrameAttached}
   278	 * - {@link PageEvent.FrameNavigated}
   279	 * - {@link PageEvent.FrameDetached}
   280	 *
   281	 * @public
   282	 */
   283	export abstract class Frame extends EventEmitter<FrameEvents> {
   284	  /**
   285	   * @internal
   286	   */
   287	  _id!: string;
   288	  /**
   289	   * @internal
   290	   */
   291	  _parentId?: string;
   292	
   293	  /**
   294	   * @internal
   295	   */
   296	  _name?: string;
   297	
   298	  /**
   299	   * @internal
   300	   */
   301	  _hasStartedLoading = false;
   302	
   303	  /**
   304	   * @internal
   305	   */
   306	  constructor() {
   307	    super();
   308	  }
   309	
   310	  /**
   311	   * The page associated with the frame.
   312	   */
   313	  abstract page(): Page;
   314	
   315	  /**
   316	   * Navigates the frame or page to the given `url`.
   317	   *
   318	   * @remarks
   319	   * Navigation to `about:blank` or navigation to the same URL with a different
   320	   * hash will succeed and return `null`.
   321	   *
   322	   * :::warning
   323	   *
   324	   * Headless shell mode doesn't support navigation to a PDF document. See the
   325	   * {@link https://crbug.com/761295 | upstream issue}.
   326	   *
   327	   * :::
   328	   *
   329	   * In headless shell, this method will not throw an error when any valid HTTP
   330	   * status code is returned by the remote server, including 404 "Not Found" and
   331	   * 500 "Internal Server Error". The status code for such responses can be
   332	   * retrieved by calling {@link HTTPResponse.status}.
   333	   *
   334	   * @param url - URL to navigate the frame to. The URL should include scheme,
   335	   * e.g. `https://`
   336	   * @param options - Options to configure waiting behavior.
   337	   * @returns A promise which resolves to the main resource response. In case of
   338	   * multiple redirects, the navigation will resolve with the response of the
   339	   * last redirect.
   340	   * @throws If:
   341	   *
   342	   * - there's an SSL error (e.g. in case of self-signed certificates).
   343	   *
   344	   * - target URL is invalid.
   345	   *
   346	   * - the timeout is exceeded during navigation.
   347	   *
   348	   * - the remote server does not respond or is unreachable.
   349	   *
   350	   * - the main resource failed to load.
   351	   */
   352	  abstract goto(
   353	    url: string,
   354	    options?: GoToOptions,
   355	  ): Promise<HTTPResponse | null>;
   356	
   357	  /**
   358	   * Waits for the frame to navigate. It is useful for when you run code which
   359	   * will indirectly cause the frame to navigate.
   360	   *
   361	   * Usage of the
   362	   * {@link https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/History_API | History API}
   363	   * to change the URL is considered a navigation.
   364	   *
   365	   * @example
   366	   *
   367	   * ```ts
   368	   * const [response] = await Promise.all([
   369	   *   // The navigation promise resolves after navigation has finished
   370	   *   frame.waitForNavigation(),
   371	   *   // Clicking the link will indirectly cause a navigation
   372	   *   frame.click('a.my-link'),
   373	   * ]);
   374	   * ```
   375	   *
   376	   * @param options - Options to configure waiting behavior.
   377	   * @returns A promise which resolves to the main resource response.
   378	   */
   379	  abstract waitForNavigation(
   380	    options?: WaitForOptions,
   381	  ): Promise<HTTPResponse | null>;
   382	
   383	  /**
   384	   * @internal
   385	   */
   386	  abstract get client(): CDPSession;
   387	
   388	  /**
   389	   * @internal
   390	   */
   391	  abstract get accessibility(): Accessibility;
   392	
   393	  /**
   394	   * @internal
   395	   */
   396	  abstract mainRealm(): Realm;
   397	
   398	  /**
   399	   * @internal
   400	   */
   401	  abstract isolatedRealm(): Realm;
   402	
   403	  #_document: Promise<ElementHandle<Document>> | undefined;
   404	
   405	  /**
   406	   * @internal
   407	   */
   408	  #document(): Promise<ElementHandle<Document>> {
   409	    if (!this.#_document) {
   410	      this.#_document = this.mainRealm().evaluateHandle(() => {
   411	        return document;
   412	      });
   413	    }
   414	    return this.#_document;
   415	  }
   416	
   417	  /**
   418	   * Used to clear the document handle that has been destroyed.
   419	   *
   420	   * @internal
   421	   */
   422	  clearDocumentHandle(): void {
   423	    this.#_document = undefined;
   424	  }
   425	
   426	  /**
   427	   * @returns The frame element associated with this frame (if any).
   428	   */
   429	  @throwIfDetached
   430	  async frameElement(): Promise<HandleFor<HTMLIFrameElement> | null> {
   431	    const parentFrame = this.parentFrame();
   432	    if (!parentFrame) {
   433	      return null;
   434	    }
   435	    using list = await parentFrame.isolatedRealm().evaluateHandle(() => {
   436	      return document.querySelectorAll('iframe,frame');
   437	    });
   438	    for await (using iframe of transposeIterableHandle(list)) {
   439	      const frame = await iframe.contentFrame();
   440	      if (frame?._id === this._id) {
   441	        return (await parentFrame
   442	          .mainRealm()
   443	          .adoptHandle(iframe)) as HandleFor<HTMLIFrameElement>;
   444	      }
   445	    }
   446	    return null;
   447	  }
   448	
   449	  /**
   450	   * Behaves identically to {@link Page.evaluateHandle} except it's run within
   451	   * the context of this frame.
   452	   *
   453	   * See {@link Page.evaluateHandle} for details.
   454	   */
   455	  @throwIfDetached
   456	  async evaluateHandle<
   457	    Params extends unknown[],
   458	    Func extends EvaluateFunc<Params> = EvaluateFunc<Params>,
   459	  >(
   460	    pageFunction: Func | string,
   461	    ...args: Params
   462	  ): Promise<HandleFor<Awaited<ReturnType<Func>>>> {
   463	    pageFunction = withSourcePuppeteerURLIfNone(
   464	      this.evaluateHandle.name,
   465	      pageFunction,
   466	    );
   467	    return await this.mainRealm().evaluateHandle(pageFunction, ...args);
   468	  }
   469	
   470	  /**
   471	   * Behaves identically to {@link Page.evaluate} except it's run within
   472	   * the context of this frame.
   473	   *
   474	   * See {@link Page.evaluate} for details.
   475	   */
   476	  @throwIfDetached
   477	  async evaluate<
   478	    Params extends unknown[],
   479	    Func extends EvaluateFunc<Params> = EvaluateFunc<Params>,
   480	  >(
   481	    pageFunction: Func | string,
   482	    ...args: Params
   483	  ): Promise<Awaited<ReturnType<Func>>> {
   484	    pageFunction = withSourcePuppeteerURLIfNone(
   485	      this.evaluate.name,
   486	      pageFunction,
   487	    );
   488	    return await this.mainRealm().evaluate(pageFunction, ...args);
   489	  }
   490	
   491	  /**
   492	   * Creates a locator for the provided selector. See {@link Locator} for
   493	   * details and supported actions.
   494	   *
   495	   * @param selector -
   496	   * {@link https://pptr.dev/guides/page-interactions#selectors | selector}
   497	   * to query the page for.
   498	   * {@link https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/CSS_Selectors | CSS selectors}
   499	   * can be passed as-is and a
   500	   * {@link https://pptr.dev/guides/page-interactions#non-css-selectors | Puppeteer-specific selector syntax}
   501	   * allows querying by
   502	   * {@link https://pptr.dev/guides/page-interactions#text-selectors--p-text | text},
   503	   * {@link https://pptr.dev/guides/page-interactions#aria-selectors--p-aria | a11y role and name},
   504	   * and
   505	   * {@link https://pptr.dev/guides/page-interactions#xpath-selectors--p-xpath | xpath}
   506	   * and
   507	   * {@link https://pptr.dev/guides/page-interactions#querying-elements-in-shadow-dom | combining these queries across shadow roots}.
   508	   * Alternatively, you can specify the selector type using a
   509	   * {@link https://pptr.dev/guides/page-interactions#prefixed-selector-syntax | prefix}.
   510	   */
   511	  locator<Selector extends string>(
   512	    selector: Selector,
   513	  ): Locator<NodeFor<Selector>>;
   514	
   515	  /**
   516	   * Creates a locator for the provided function. See {@link Locator} for
   517	   * details and supported actions.
   518	   */
   519	  locator<Ret>(func: () => Awaitable<Ret>): Locator<Ret>;
   520	
   521	  /**
   522	   * @internal
   523	   */
   524	  @throwIfDetached
   525	  locator<Selector extends string, Ret, T extends Node>(
   526	    input: Selector | (() => Awaitable<Ret>),
   527	  ): Locator<NodeFor<Selector>> | Locator<Ret> | Locator<T> {
   528	    if (typeof input === 'string') {
   529	      return NodeLocator.create(this, input);
   530	    } else {
   531	      return FunctionLocator.create(this, input);
   532	    }
   533	  }
   534	  /**
   535	   * Queries the frame for an element matching the given selector.
   536	   *
   537	   * @param selector -
   538	   * {@link https://pptr.dev/guides/page-interactions#selectors | selector}
   539	   * to query the page for.
   540	   * {@link https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/CSS_Selectors | CSS selectors}
   541	   * can be passed as-is and a
   542	   * {@link https://pptr.dev/guides/page-interactions#non-css-selectors | Puppeteer-specific selector syntax}
   543	   * allows querying by
   544	   * {@link https://pptr.dev/guides/page-interactions#text-selectors--p-text | text},
   545	   * {@link https://pptr.dev/guides/page-interactions#aria-selectors--p-aria | a11y role and name},
   546	   * and
   547	   * {@link https://pptr.dev/guides/page-interactions#xpath-selectors--p-xpath | xpath}
   548	   * and
   549	   * {@link https://pptr.dev/guides/page-interactions#querying-elements-in-shadow-dom | combining these queries across shadow roots}.
   550	   * Alternatively, you can specify the selector type using a
   551	   * {@link https://pptr.dev/guides/page-interactions#prefixed-selector-syntax | prefix}.
   552	   *
   553	   * @returns A {@link ElementHandle | element handle} to the first element
   554	   * matching the given selector. Otherwise, <response clipped><NOTE>To save on context only part of this file has been shown to you. You should retry this tool after you have searched inside the file with `grep -n` in order to find the line numbers of what you are looking for.</NOTE>

const browser = await puppeteer.launch({headless: true, args: ['--no-sandbox']});

Let me look at what the PR description says more carefully: "Expose Generic Autofill Support via CDP (Autofill.trigger)". The PR says "Puppeteer currently provides only limited support around autofill (mainly focused on credit card scenarios), and there is no clear high-level API that exposes the full capabilities of Autofill.trigger."

So the error is "Field not found" from the CDP. This might be because the form doesn't have `autocomplete` attributes, which are needed for the browser to know which field is which. Let me look at the HTML again:

The HTML form has:
- `<input size="40" id="name" />`
- `<input size="40" id="number" name="card_number" />`
- `<input size="2" id="expiration_month" name="ccmonth">`

Let me check if there's a way to make this work by looking at the existing compiled type definitions and source to see if there are differences:

The source `AutofillData` (in `src/api/ElementHandle.ts`):
```typescript
export interface AutofillData {
  creditCard: {
    number: string;
    name: string;
    expiryMonth: string;
    expiryYear: string;
    cvc: string;
  };
}
```

The CDP `TriggerRequest`:
```typescript
export interface TriggerRequest {
    fieldId: DOM.BackendNodeId;
    frameId?: Page.FrameId;
    card?: CreditCard;
    address?: Address;
}
```

So to expose "Generic Autofill Support", we need to update `AutofillData` to also support `address`. And update the implementation to pass `address` when provided.

```typescript
});
```

- `Autofill.trigger` - Trigger autofill on a form
- `Autofill.setAddresses` - Set addresses so that developers can verify their forms implementation
- `Autofill.enable` - Enables autofill domain notifications
- `Autofill.disable` - Disables autofill domain notifications

2026-05-30 11:16:47,105 - INFO - swea-agent-puppeteer__puppeteer-14826 - 🤖 MODEL INPUT
OBSERVATION:

# Copyright 2017 The Chromium Authors
# Use of this source code is governed by a BSD-style license that can be
# found in the LICENSE file.
#
# Contributing to Chrome DevTools Protocol: https://goo.gle/devtools-contribution-guide-cdp

# Defines commands and events for Autofill.
experimental domain Autofill
  type CreditCard extends object
    properties
      # 16-digit credit card number.
      string number
      # Name of the credit card owner.
      string name
      # 2-digit expiry month.
      string expiryMonth
      # 4-digit expiry year.
      string expiryYear
      # 3-digit card verification code.
      string cvc

  type AddressField extends object
    properties
      # address field name, for example GIVEN_NAME.
      # The full list of supported field names:
      # https://source.chromium.org/chromium/chromium/src/+/main:components/autofill/core/browser/field_types.cc;l=38
      string name
      # address field value, for example Jon Doe.
      string value

  # A list of address fields.
  type AddressFields extends object
    properties
      array of AddressField fields

  type Address extends object
    properties
      # fields and values defining an address.
      array of AddressField fields

  # Defines how an address can be displayed like in chrome://settings/addresses.
  # Address UI is a two dimensional array, each inner array is an "address information line", and when rendered in a UI surface should be displayed as such.
  # The following address UI for instance:
  # [[{name: "GIVE_NAME", value: "Jon"}, {name: "FAMILY_NAME", value: "Doe"}], [{name: "CITY", value: "Munich"}, {name: "ZIP", value: "81456"}]]
  # should allow the receiver to render:
  # Jon Doe
  # Munich 81456
  type AddressUI extends object
    properties
      # A two dimension array containing the representation of values from an address profile.
      array of AddressFields addressFields

  # Specified whether a filled field was done so by using the html autocomplete attribute or autofill heuristics.
  type FillingStrategy extends string
    enum
      autocompleteAttribute
      autofillInferred

  type FilledField extends object
    properties
      # The type of the field, e.g text, password etc.
      string htmlType
      # the html id
      string id
      # the html name
      string name
      # the field value
      string value
      # The actual field type, e.g FAMILY_NAME
      string autofillType
      # The filling strategy
      FillingStrategy fillingStrategy
      # The frame the field belongs to
      Page.FrameId frameId
      # The form field's DOM node
      DOM.BackendNodeId fieldId

  # Emitted when an address form is filled.
  event addressFormFilled
    parameters
      # Information about the fields that were filled
      array of FilledField filledFields
      # An UI representation of the address used to fill the form.
      # Consists of a 2D array where each child represents an address/profile line.
      AddressUI addressUi

  # Trigger autofill on a form identified by the fieldId.
  # If the field and related form cannot be autofilled, returns an error.
  command trigger
    parameters
      # Identifies a field that serves as an anchor for autofill.
      DOM.BackendNodeId fieldId
      # Identifies the frame that field belongs to.
      optional Page.FrameId frameId
      # Credit card information to fill out the form. Credit card data is not saved.  Mutually exclusive with `address`.
      optional CreditCard card
      # Address to fill out the form. Address data is not saved. Mutually exclusive with `card`.
      optional Address address

  # Set addresses so that developers can verify their forms implementation.
  command setAddresses
    # Test addresses for the available countries.
    parameters
      array of Address addresses

  # Disables autofill domain notifications.
  command disable

  # Enables autofill domain notifications.
  command enable

Let me look at what changes might be needed to make `Autofill.trigger` work. The test HTML form doesn't have `autocomplete` attributes. According to the Chromium autofill documentation, the form fields need proper `autocomplete` attributes for the browser to recognize them. Let me check if the test HTML needs to be updated:

The current HTML:
```html
<input size="40" id="name" />
<input size="40" id="number" name="card_number" />
<input size="2" id="expiration_month" name="ccmonth">
<input size="4" id="expiration_year" name="ccyear" />
```

For credit card autofill to work, the fields should have attributes like:
- `autocomplete="cc-name"` for name
- `autocomplete="cc-number"` for card number
- `autocomplete="cc-exp-month"` for expiration month
- `autocomplete="cc-exp-year"` for expiration year

But the PR says: "I've already taken care of all changes to any of the test files described in the <pr_description>."

The PR description doesn't describe any specific test files. It only describes the feature. So maybe the test files are fine as-is and the issue is just the source code.

- `AutofillData` interface only has `creditCard`

- `AutofillData` should also support `address` (with `fields` array)

cd /testbed && timeout 30 node -e "
import puppeteer from 'puppeteer';

const browser = await puppeteer.launch({
  headless: true,
  args: ['--no-sandbox', '--disable-setuid-sandbox'],
});
const page = await browser.newPage();
await page.setContent(\`
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<body>
  <form id='testform' method='post'>
    <input size='40' id='name' autocomplete='cc-name' />
    <input size='40' id='number' name='card_number' autocomplete='cc-number' />
    <input size='2' id='expiration_month' name='ccmonth' autocomplete='cc-exp-month' />
    <input size='4' id='expiration_year' name='ccyear' autocomplete='cc-exp-year' />
    <input type='submit' value='Submit'>
  </form>
</body>
</html>
\`);
const name = await page.waitForSelector('#name');
try {
  await name.autofill({
    creditCard: {
      number: '4444444444444444',
      name: 'John Smith',
      expiryMonth: '01',
      expiryYear: '2030',
      cvc: '123',
    },
  });
  const result = await page.evaluate(() => {
    const result = [];
    for (const el of document.querySelectorAll('input')) {
      result.push(el.value);
    }
    return result.join(',');
  });
  console.log('Result:', result);
  console.log('Expected: John Smith,4444444444444444,01,2030,Submit');
  console.log('Match:', result === 'John Smith,4444444444444444,01,2030,Submit');
} catch (e) {
  console.log('Error:', e.message);
}
await browser.close();

import puppeteer from 'puppeteer';

const browser = await puppeteer.launch({
  headless: true,
  args: ['--no-sandbox', '--disable-setuid-sandbox'],
});
const page = await browser.newPage();
const client = await page.createCDPSession();
await client.send('Autofill.enable');

await page.setContent(`
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<body>
  <form id="testform" method="post">
    <input size="40" id="name" autocomplete="cc-name" />
    <input size="40" id="number" name="card_number" autocomplete="cc-number" />
    <input size="2" id="expiration_month" name="ccmonth" autocomplete="cc-exp-month" />
    <input size="4" id="expiration_year" name="ccyear" autocomplete="cc-exp-year" />
    <input type="submit" value="Submit">
  </form>
</body>
</html>
`);
const name = await page.waitForSelector('#name');
try {
  await name.autofill({
    creditCard: {
      number: '4444444444444444',
      name: 'John Smith',
      expiryMonth: '01',
      expiryYear: '2030',
      cvc: '123',
    },
  });
  const result = await page.evaluate(() => {
    const result = [];
    for (const el of document.querySelectorAll('input')) {
      result.push(el.value);
    }
    return result.join(',');
  });
  console.log('Result:', result);
  console.log('Expected: John Smith,4444444444444444,01,2030,Submit');
  console.log('Match:', result === 'John Smith,4444444444444444,01,2030,Submit');
} catch (e) {
  console.log('Error:', e.message);
}
await browser.close();

import puppeteer from 'puppeteer';

const browser = await puppeteer.launch({
  headless: true,
  args: ['--no-sandbox', '--disable-setuid-sandbox'],
});
const page = await browser.newPage();
const client = await page.createCDPSession();

await page.setContent(`
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<body>
  <form id="testform" method="post">
    <input size="40" id="name" autocomplete="cc-name" />
    <input size="40" id="number" name="card_number" autocomplete="cc-number" />
    <input size="2" id="expiration_month" name="ccmonth" autocomplete="cc-exp-month" />
    <input size="4" id="expiration_year" name="ccyear" autocomplete="cc-exp-year" />
    <input type="submit" value="Submit">
  </form>
</body>
</html>
`);

// Get the document root
const {root} = await client.send('DOM.getDocument', {depth: -1});
console.log('Root nodeId:', root.nodeId);

// Find the name input
const {nodeId} = await client.send('DOM.querySelector', {
  nodeId: root.nodeId,
  selector: '#name'
});
console.log('Name nodeId:', nodeId);

const nodeInfo = await client.send('DOM.describeNode', {
  nodeId: nodeId,
});
console.log('Node info:', JSON.stringify(nodeInfo.node, null, 2));

const frameTree = await client.send('Page.getFrameTree');
const frameId = frameTree.frameTree.frame.id;
console.log('FrameId:', frameId);

try {
  await client.send('Autofill.trigger', {
    fieldId: nodeInfo.node.backendNodeId,
    frameId: frameId,
    card: {
      number: '4444444444444444',
      name: 'John Smith',
      expiryMonth: '01',
      expiryYear: '2030',
      cvc: '123',
    },
  });
  const result = await page.evaluate(() => {
    const result = [];
    for (const el of document.querySelectorAll('input')) {
      result.push(el.value);
    }
    return result.join(',');
  });
  console.log('Result:', result);
} catch (e) {
  console.log('Error:', e.message);
}
await browser.close();

cd /testbed && timeout 30 node << 'EOF'
import puppeteer from 'puppeteer';

const browser = await puppeteer.launch({
  headless: true,
  args: ['--no-sandbox', '--disable-setuid-sandbox'],
});
const page = await browser.newPage();
const client = await page.createCDPSession();
await client.send('Autofill.enable');

await page.setContent(`
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<body>
  <form id="testform" method="post">
    <input size="40" id="name" autocomplete="cc-name" />
    <input size="40" id="number" name="card_number" autocomplete="cc-number" />
    <input size="2" id="expiration_month" name="ccmonth" autocomplete="cc-exp-month" />
    <input size="4" id="expiration_year" name="ccyear" autocomplete="cc-exp-year" />
    <input type="submit" value="Submit">
  </form>
</body>
</html>
`);

// Get the document root
const {root} = await client.send('DOM.getDocument', {depth: -1});
const {nodeId} = await client.send('DOM.querySelector', {
  nodeId: root.nodeId,
  selector: '#name'
});
const nodeInfo = await client.send('DOM.describeNode', {
  nodeId: nodeId,
});

const frameTree = await client.send('Page.getFrameTree');
const frameId = frameTree.frameTree.frame.id;

try {
  await client.send('Autofill.trigger', {
    fieldId: nodeInfo.node.backendNodeId,
    frameId: frameId,
    card: {
      number: '4444444444444444',
      name: 'John Smith',
      expiryMonth: '01',
      expiryYear: '2030',
      cvc: '123',
    },
  });
  const result = await page.evaluate(() => {
    const result = [];
    for (const el of document.querySelectorAll('input')) {
      result.push(el.value);
    }
    return result.join(',');
  });
  console.log('Result:', result);
} catch (e) {
  console.log('Error:', e.message);
}
await browser.close();
EOF

import puppeteer from 'puppeteer';

const browser = await puppeteer.launch({
  headless: true,
  args: ['--no-sandbox', '--disable-setuid-sandbox'],
});
const page = await browser.newPage();
const client = await page.createCDPSession();
await client.send('Autofill.enable');

await page.setContent(`
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<body>
  <form id="testform" method="post">
    <input id="name" autocomplete="name" />
    <input id="street" autocomplete="street-address" />
    <input id="city" autocomplete="address-level2" />
    <input id="zip" autocomplete="postal-code" />
    <input type="submit" value="Submit">
  </form>
</body>
</html>
`);

const {root} = await client.send('DOM.getDocument', {depth: -1});
const {nodeId} = await client.send('DOM.querySelector', {
  nodeId: root.nodeId,
  selector: '#name'
});
const nodeInfo = await client.send('DOM.describeNode', {
  nodeId: nodeId,
});

const frameTree = await client.send('Page.getFrameTree');
const frameId = frameTree.frameTree.frame.id;

// Try with address
try {
  await client.send('Autofill.trigger', {
    fieldId: nodeInfo.node.backendNodeId,
    frameId: frameId,
    address: {
      fields: [
        {name: 'NAME_FULL', value: 'John Smith'},
      ]
    },
  });
  const result = await page.evaluate(() => {
    const result = [];
    for (const el of document.querySelectorAll('input')) {
      result.push(el.value);
    }
    return result.join(',');
  });
  console.log('Result:', result);
} catch (e) {
  console.log('Error:', e.message);
}
await browser.close();

import puppeteer from 'puppeteer';

const browser = await puppeteer.launch({
  headless: false,
  args: ['--no-sandbox', '--disable-setuid-sandbox'],
});
const page = await browser.newPage();
const client = await page.createCDPSession();
await client.send('Autofill.enable');

await page.setContent(`
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<body>
  <form id="testform" method="post">
    <input size="40" id="name" autocomplete="cc-name" />
    <input size="40" id="number" name="card_number" autocomplete="cc-number" />
    <input size="2" id="expiration_month" name="ccmonth" autocomplete="cc-exp-month" />
    <input size="4" id="expiration_year" name="ccyear" autocomplete="cc-exp-year" />
    <input type="submit" value="Submit">
  </form>
</body>
</html>
`);

const {root} = await client.send('DOM.getDocument', {depth: -1});
const {nodeId} = await client.send('DOM.querySelector', {
  nodeId: root.nodeId,
  selector: '#name'
});
const nodeInfo = await client.send('DOM.describeNode', {
  nodeId: nodeId,
});

const frameTree = await client.send('Page.getFrameTree');
const frameId = frameTree.frameTree.frame.id;

try {
  await client.send('Autofill.trigger', {
    fieldId: nodeInfo.node.backendNodeId,
    frameId: frameId,
    card: {
      number: '4444444444444444',
      name: 'John Smith',
      expiryMonth: '01',
      expiryYear: '2030',
      cvc: '123',
    },
  });
  const result = await page.evaluate(() => {
    const result = [];
    for (const el of document.querySelectorAll('input')) {
      result.push(el.value);
    }
    return result.join(',');
  });
  console.log('Result:', result);
} catch (e) {
  console.log('Error:', e.message);
}
await browser.close();
EOF

export declare interface AutofillData {
  /**
   * See {@link https://chromedevtools.github.io/devtools-protocol/tot/Autofill/#type-CreditCard | Autofill.CreditCard}.
   */
  creditCard: {

    expiryYear: string;

import puppeteer from 'puppeteer';

const browser = await puppeteer.launch({
  headless: true,
  args: ['--no-sandbox', '--disable-setuid-sandbox'],
});
const page = await browser.newPage();
const client = await page.createCDPSession();

await page.goto('about:blank');
await page.setContent(`
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<body>
  <form id="testform" method="post">
    <input size="40" id="name" autocomplete="cc-name" />
    <input size="40" id="number" name="card_number" autocomplete="cc-number" />
    <input size="2" id="expiration_month" name="ccmonth" autocomplete="cc-exp-month" />
    <input size="4" id="expiration_year" name="ccyear" autocomplete="cc-exp-year" />
    <input type="submit" value="Submit">
  </form>
</body>
</html>
`);
await page.waitForSelector('#name');

// Try using the ElementHandle.autofill
const name = await page.$('#name');
try {
  await name.autofill({
    creditCard: {
      number: '4444444444444444',
      name: 'John Smith',
      expiryMonth: '01',
      expiryYear: '2030',
      cvc: '123',
    },
  });
  const result = await page.evaluate(() => {
    const result = [];
    for (const el of document.querySelectorAll('input')) {
      result.push(el.value);
    }
    return result.join(',');
  });
  console.log('Result:', result);
} catch (e) {
  console.log('Error:', e.message);
}
await browser.close();

Let me look at the problem from a fresh perspective. The PR says "Expose Generic Autofill Support via CDP (Autofill.trigger)". What changes are needed to the source code to fully expose the CDP `Autofill.trigger` capabilities?

The CDP `Autofill.trigger` accepts:
- `fieldId` (required)
- `frameId` (optional)
- `card` (optional) - credit card info
- `address` (optional) - address info

The current Puppeteer API:
- `AutofillData` interface with only `creditCard` field
- Implementation passes `card: data.creditCard`

To fully expose generic autofill, we need to:
1. Update `AutofillData` to also include `address` field (mutually exclusive with `creditCard`)
2. Update the implementations (CDP and Bidi) to pass `address` when provided

Let me also check if there's a `Page.autofill` or `Frame.autofill` method that should exist but doesn't. Let me search:
